summary: Iteration 8 covered (a) regression that the new background push triggers (Emergent relay) do NOT block the order lifecycle, (b) presence + Pydantic validation of POST /api/register-push, and (c) frontend in-app lifecycle banner rendering on shipper-tracking. 30/30 backend tests passed (11 new + 19 existing P0 state-machine). Backend logs confirmed push triggers fire in background and fail with 'EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid' (placeholder env) WITHOUT affecting primary endpoint responses — exactly as designed. Frontend Expo web preview loaded cleanly with no crash from expo-audio / expo-notifications; the floating lifecycle banner was visually verified rendering with correct title 'Driver at pickup' on the shipper-tracking screen, and underlying page state transitioned correctly to Driver Assigned, Driver Arrived, and Delivered.

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket wss://.../ws/track/{id} fails with 502 in the preview environment (likely k8s ingress not configured for WS upgrade). Polling fallback still works so tracking is functional, but real-time updates would be slower than designed. Pre-existing — unchanged this iteration.

action_items: Optional: in NotificationContext useEffect on shipper-tracking, the `prev === null` guard suppresses the very first transition the user lands on. If a shipper opens tracking AFTER the driver has already accepted, no 'Driver assigned' banner fires. Current behaviour is reasonable (prevents stale notifications on re-open) but worth confirming with product.
Optional: investigate the 502 on the /ws/track/{id} WebSocket upgrade in the preview ingress so the realtime path is also exercised; polling fallback already keeps the UI live.
critical_code_review_comments: Push triggers use asyncio.create_task wrapped in try/except inside send_push() — verified at runtime that an upstream 401 from the relay only logs a warning and does NOT raise into the order endpoints. Pattern is correct.
POST /api/register-push relies on Pydantic for body validation (returns 422) before any relay call — confirmed.
alerts.ts uses expo-audio (the modern, non-deprecated package). Defensive require() wrapping ensures a missing module never crashes the JS bundle. Web has no haptics — also guarded.
Backend file server.py is 4228 lines — still flagged from prior iterations as oversized; recommend splitting (drivers, shipper, orders, push) into routers.
seed_data_creation: Used existing POST /api/seed-demo. New test fixtures create disposable shipments prefixed with 'TEST' to avoid polluting demo data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push notification feature: backend endpoint /api/register-push exists with proper 422 validation; with EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY=placeholder the upstream relay rejects valid bodies with 401 and our endpoint returns 500 — this is EXPECTED per the request and not a bug. Background push triggers on accept/advance/create are fire-and-forget (asyncio.create_task) and proven NOT to affect order lifecycle responses. In-app banners (shipper-tracking + driver-home) are wired via NotificationContext + alerts.ts and verified to render in the web preview. WebSocket /ws/track/{id} returns 502 in this preview env (pre-existing); polling fallback works.
